senses.gloss: to break (a treaty, covenant, promise, etc), to renege

examples.text: ٱلَّذِينَ يَنقُضُونَ عَهْدَ ٱللَّهِ مِن بَعْدِ مِيثَـٰقِهِ وَيَقْطَعُونَ مَآ أَمَرَ ٱللَّهُ بِهِ أَن يُوصَلَ وَيُفْسِدُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰسِرُونَ
examples.english: those who violate Allah’s covenant after it has been affirmed, break whatever ˹ties˺ Allah has ordered to be maintained, and spread corruption in the land. It is they who are the ˹true˺ losers.

senses.gloss: to cut, to split, to slit open, to crack

etymology.text:
Morphologically from the root ن ق ض (n q ḍ).

See the etymology of the corresponding lemma form.
